You got your main book that you're working on (I'm assuming that’s what you meant by one book at a time),  and then you have your comfort book for when you had a bad day and just want to dissapear on the purple line.

lines.speaker: paradox

lines.speaker: drifter
lines.transcription: Thank you kindly, miss.

lines.speaker: paradox

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That's not particulalry helpful.

lines.speaker: paradox

lines.transcription: I am absolutely fascinated by your organization Paradox

lines.speaker: paradox

lines.speaker: paradox
lines.transcription: I think you'll enjoy our next event, I'll make sure you get in.

lines.speaker: dynamo

lines.transcription: Point taken.

lines.transcription: I wouldn't know, art's not really my thing.

lines.transcription: I want it back.

lines.transcription: I am not someone to play games with.

lines.transcription: You know I've been to the Paradox Museum last summer.

lines.speaker: paradox

lines.transcription: I found it cliché and overwrought.

lines.transcription: How often does your little social club gather

lines.transcription: I'm assuming your ilk like to brag about what they stole?

lines.transcription: For all the might your organization wields, you waste it on publicity stunts.

lines.speaker: paradox
lines.transcription: We don't need to explain ourselves to you.

lines.speaker: seven

lines.transcription: I won't be threatened by you Seven

lines.transcription: Does Paradox take comissions?

lines.transcription: But if I proposed  something… would you consider it?

lines.speaker: paradox
lines.transcription: I would be lying if I didn't say I was curious

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: We'll speak later.

lines.transcription: Have you ever considered being an adjunct professor?

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: No, I'd make a really bad teacher.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Why do you think that?

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: I don't know how I do what I do, it just comes naturally… if I tried to teach someone it would probably boil down to me saying "try it again, but don't suck this time"

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Good god, you'd be the Lash of acadameia.

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: You say it like that, I kind of wanna give it a try.

conversations.summary: Discussing McGinnis potentially becoming adjunct professor.

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: Neat gun.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Thanks, I desgined it myself.

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: Did you patent it?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Oh no, why do you ask?

lines.speaker: mcginnis
lines.transcription: No reason.

conversations.summary: Discussion about gun design and patent status.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: May I have a word professor?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Of course

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Your sport coat is wretched

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Not sure what to do with that

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Find dignity and a halfway decent tailor

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'll see what I can do.

conversations.summary: Lady Geist critiques Dynamo's poor fashion choice.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That binding ward is looking in rough shape

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: A fact that I'm keenly aware of.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I would have that looked at soon.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Thank you for your concern professor.

conversations.summary: Concern over deteriorating binding ward condition.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: You seem preoccupied professor

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Just thinking about my wife

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Is she alive?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Yes.  And very paitient.  Hopfully after the ritual that patience will be rearded.

conversations.summary: Dynamo reflects on wife and upcoming ritual.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Pleasure to see you again, Lady Geist.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Thank you for your help, Professor.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Thank you for the funding. I trust that your Oath Keeper situation has been resolved.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: I would say mitigated to my satisfaction.

conversations.summary: Acknowledgment of help and funding resolution.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: You're a man of science, Professor. Do you think what they say about the patrons is true?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well, it's clear that the Patrons are beings of immense power. Whether or not they actually intend to fulfill their end of the bargain is less about science and more about their nature. On the plus side, we're about to find out.

conversations.summary: Discussing Patrons' power and true intentions.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: I don't understand you, professor… you've been bestowed a great gift, and you're willing to throw it all away for nothing.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Easy words to say when you've never loved someone.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: No.  Easy words to say when you have.

conversations.summary: Love's sacrifice debated between professor and friend.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: I'm a fan of your work Professor.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I am flattered and horrified in equal measure

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: Your research into the Astral Gates paved the way for my own.  In my rare moments of doubt, sometimes I even wonder if I would have became the man I am were it not for you.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: and now I'm just horrifeid.

conversations.summary: Admiration and self-doubt about influential research.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm sure it's a touchy subject, but I simply must know how you stopped your soul from being obliterated

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: I mean no disrespect Professor, but some things may be beyond you.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Many things are, but if I let that stop me from trying to learn I wouldn't be.. Well I probably would still have my body.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: Hm.  Perhaps we're cut from the same cloth.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Let's not go that far.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: I'll consider your request, Professor.

conversations.summary: Discussing protecting soul from obliteration.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: Marlowe wanted me to tell you that the offer still stands.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: For a moment I thought you said Marla and I got terribly confused.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: It's a good offer.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm quite happy in the private sector.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: You change your mind let me know.

conversations.summary: Job offer from Marlowe discussed, declined initially.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: So you're in the Sandman Division.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: Yes.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Are there a lot of you?

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: Enough that we call ourselves a divison.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That's a rather unsettling idea.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: We like it that way.

conversations.summary: Sandman Division size and unsettling reputation discussed.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Once I open the blackhole I'm going to be pretty exposed.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: Don't worry professor, I'll do what I can to capitalize on the opportunity.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm not worried about you capitalizing on the opportunity, I'm worried about dying in the process.

lines.speaker: haze
lines.transcription: Your surival may not be a top priority, but it is still a priority.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Great…

conversations.summary: Risk of danger opening black hole discussed.

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: Hold them in place Professor, and leave the rest to me.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That sound's rather ominous and I'm ok not asking questions

conversations.summary: Vindicta plans action; Dynamo feels uneasy.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You know I have a friend in the history department that would be over the moon if he got to meet you

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: I'm not a prop for show and tell

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You are not and I am sorry to offend you.

conversations.summary: Vindicta refuses to meet dynamo's friend.

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: You're a professor at Columbia, yes?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Indeed I am

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: Isn't that where John Hathorne will be speaking soon?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Regretablly.

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: If the ritual fails, we'll be speaking later.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Ok.

conversations.summary: Columbia professor discusses Haythorne's ritual failure.

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: I'd buy you a drink when this is all over but…

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: No mouth.  I get it.

conversations.summary: Infernus offers drink; Dynamo notes no mouth.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: May I ask a favor Infernus?

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: Sure.  We surive this I figure I'll owe you one.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I was thinking of buying a hat and I would love it if you'd join me at the haberdashery.

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: Professor, you don't gotta waste a favor on that, I got you.

conversations.summary: Dynamo requests Infernus to shop hats.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You don't seem particularly nervous

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: Not my first time lighting things on fire.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That's good.  I think.

conversations.summary: Infernus experienced; lighting fires not nervous.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Haven't seen you since the Black Chateau.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well, you've been a little busy being dead.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: It certainly does cut down on socializing.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: It's great to see you again Kelvin.

conversations.summary: Reunion after Kelvin's death at Black Chateau.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: This story writes itself: two men of science fighting to get our lives back.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Today wil be a day to remember, my friend.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Indeed it will.

conversations.summary: Scientists battling to reclaim lives, memorable day.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: How's Marla doing?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Good.  Our anniversary's coming up.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Wonderful!  How long?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: 31 years.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: You picked out a gift yet?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well, I'm hoping my body.  But if not, I've got an eye on some books.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: You won't have to settle, Professor.  We'll win this day.

conversations.summary: Dynamo's anniversary plans and gift ideas.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: Professor, you teach at Columbia yes?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That's right

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: I don't suppose you have any connections in the art department.  Mo was hoping to audit a pottery class.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Sadly I don't.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: Ah well, I guess we'll fall back on the orginal plan of threatening someone in the admissions office

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You know what, actually I do think there is someone I can talk to

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: You've made his day Professor, thank you

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Mo. Krill.  If we get our timing down I am confident that we can make their lives a living hell.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: We will endeavor not to grab someone in your black hole.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: And I'll try not to drop a black hole on someone you grabbed.

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Gentlemen, you're both well connected individuals right?

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: We have lived quite the life, yes.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: My anniversary is coming  up and I was really hoping to get Marla something special.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: What are you thinking? Jewels, fine art, Ixian trickets?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: She's a big reader so I was thinking a rare book.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: My friend, what if I told you I knew someone looking to move a first edition Don Quixote

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I would be very excited, then remind you that I have a teacher's salary.

lines.speaker: mo&krill
lines.transcription: That does make it a challenge, but one I gladly accept.  I will find you options.

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: If Columbia ever needs an adjunct professor to teach a course in being amazing, The Lash is happy to oblige.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm shocked you know what an adjunct is.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Don't underestimate The Lash professor… it never works out.

conversations.summary: Lash jokes about adjunct professor opportunity.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: So how long you been with the ol' ball and chain.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Don't talk about Marla like that.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Just making conversation.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: And I'm just telling you to respect my wife.

conversations.summary: Respecting wife during casual conversation about marriage.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: What's the first thing you're gonna do once The Lash gets you your body back.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I believe you mean "The Patron", but to answer your question: give Marla a hug and not let go for about 3 months.

conversations.summary: Plans after The Lash regains his body.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I have wanted to speak to a djinn for quite some time, I imagine they are a facisning people.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: They're people.  Some are facsinating, some are…are…

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: banal?

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: What does that word mean?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: It’s a fancy way of saying boring.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: No.  That's not it.   What do you say when somone is cruel as child would be

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Asshole.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: Yes!  Asshole.  Some are facinating.  Some are assholes.

conversations.summary: Djinn personalities: fascinating or unpleasant, like people.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: Professor, would you be willing to give Nashala and I a tour of Columbia?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: It would be my pleasure.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: Thank you.

conversations.summary: Request and acceptance for Colombian tour.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: What's your favorite part of New York that you've seen?

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: We haven't had the opportunity to do much sightseeing, but the library was stunning.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: One of my favoirte places to go think.

lines.speaker: mirage
lines.transcription: I can see why.

conversations.summary: Discussing favorite New York sightseeing spots.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I don't want to intrude-

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: Then don't.

conversations.summary: Dynamo seeks permission; Grey Talon refuses.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: You have courage professor

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: No more than most

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: A lot more than most.  You endured something horrible and yet you live your life like it's going to be ok.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: It was either that or give into despair, and that's not really an option is it?

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: I wish I could be like you.

conversations.summary: Courage and resilience in facing adversity.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: Have you been to Ixia professor?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I won't lie - after what happened to my body I decided to stay clear of Astral Gates, but I hear that South Ixia is beautiful.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: It is.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Maybe when this is over we could takes the wives on a couples trip.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: I don't think so, but thank you for the offer.

conversations.summary: Discussion about visiting Ixia and trip plans.

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: Hold 'em for me Professor, and I'll take care of the rest.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Sounds like a plan.

conversations.summary: Shiv requests help; Dynamo agrees to assist.

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: You got the stomach for this professor?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: If you think academics lack willpower you are sorely mistaken.

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: I hope so.

conversations.summary: Academic determination and willpower discussion.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Stay close to me Shiv, I'll do my best to keep you in the fight

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: Profesor, you couldn't keep me out of the fight.

conversations.summary: Encouragement and determination to keep fighting.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: After the ritual, I don't suppose I could thake a look at that case?

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: I'm afraid not

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: While disapointed, I respect your decision.

conversations.summary: Dynamo requests case; Pocket refuses permission.

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: Are you still friends with my father?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: These days "friends" might be pushing it, but I'd say he has warm nostaliga for me

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: He's not a good man.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: He's a complicated man.  There's a difference.

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: Not to me.

conversations.summary: Tense discussion about Pocket's father's character.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You should have told me you were alive, Arin.

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: Why's that.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm your godfather,  I would have helped you.

lines.speaker: pocket
lines.transcription: I'm sorry, I just… I wasn't sure who I could trust.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well at least I'm here now.

conversations.summary: Godfather reveals himself, offers support to Arin.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: It's nice to meet you Professor, Sophia speaks very highly of you.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: That's kind of you to say.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: It's just the truth.

conversations.summary: Meeting introduction and mutual respect expressed.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You're Sophia Arroyo's sister, right?

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Yeah, everything ok?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Ok-ish.  She's a great student, but lately she's been falling asleep in class.  She she says she's been pulling long shifts at her job and it's wearing her out.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: I didn't even know Sophia had a job.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well, if you could talk to her I think it'd do a world of good

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: I will, thanks.

conversations.summary: Concern about Sofia's fatigue and job.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: So Ivy, how long have you been a vigilante.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Oh, my whole life.  It was a calling, but with Mendoza gone I think I'm ready to find a career.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well, if we survive and you need a reference, let me know.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Thanks Professor.

conversations.summary: Ivy considers career change after vigilante life.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well aren't you a facinating creature…

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: I think so.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: When the ritual is over I'd love to pick your brain.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: Please don't stab my face

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Oh!  It's an expression, it means to ask questions.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: Oh, that sounds more fun.  OK!

conversations.summary: Curious dialogue about asking questions safely.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: What happened to your body?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Obliterated in an astral gate.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: That sounds upsetting.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I do not recommend it.

conversations.summary: Dynamo's body harmed by Obliterator, Astral Gate.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: So this adversary…

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: Is dangerous.  And it's only a matter of time before he tires of The Deep and comes for you on land.

conversations.summary: Adversary poses land threat after sea exhaustion.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You seem impressively dour.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Indeed, but I found a positive outlook makes all the difference.

conversations.summary: Dynamo advises Warden to stay positive.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I appreciate the sentiment, Warden. But don't worry about me. I'll be beside you, not behind you.

conversations.summary: [Not enough transcribed content for summary]

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Warden, I wanted to put your mind at ease. I'm not just a man of science, but a man of action.

conversations.summary: [Not enough transcribed content for summary]

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I couldn't help but overhear one of my students mention that she frequents your establishment.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: This may surprise you Professor, but college kids like to have fun.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Indeed they do.  They also don't have fully formed brains, and if I find out you are taking advantage of that I will it take it personally.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: Ok dad.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Tred carefully wratih.

conversations.summary: Concern over student safety at Wraith's place.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: I don't suppose you're a gambling man Professor?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Marla and I have been known to make bridge night a little more interesting.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: Not a poker guy?

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I prefer working with people, not lying to them.

conversations.summary: Dynamo prefers honest games over gambling deceit.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Wraith, you're a killer, right?

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: I mean I've killed people but it's not like a recreational thing for me.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: So yes.  Anyway: a profesional question for you -

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: again, not a proffessional

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: In your line of work do people end up dead

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: Oh yeah

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Because of you.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: I am often adjacent, yes.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Then close enough.  The point is if I hold someone in a black hole are you ok with shooting them.

lines.speaker: wraith
lines.transcription: Honey, I got you covered don't worry.

conversations.summary: Discussing lethal outcomes and shooting consent.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: You're a repugnant wrecker.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Coming from you, I'll take that as a compliment.

conversations.summary: Insult exchanged, interpreted as a compliment.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Forgive me, I don't speak war criminal.

conversations.summary: [Not enough transcribed content for summary]

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I wonder where that patron put us together.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Well now you hurt my feelings.

conversations.summary: Dynamo feels hurt by Wrecker's comment.

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: in japanese " We need to come up with a plan" - 計画を立てなくては

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Yes.

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: in japanese " You don't know what I said do you." - どうせわかってないだろう？

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Sounds good.

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: in japanese " Oh god." - まったく。。。

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: We got this.

conversations.summary: Planning discussion with some frustration expressed.

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: in japanese "Your ego brought your curse upon you." - お前のエゴが招いた結果だ

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: I'm going to assume that's a compliment

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: It wasn't.

conversations.summary: Conflict over ego and unintended consequences.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Forgive my ignorance Yamato, but is that sword an heirloom?

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: It is.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: How old?

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: Very.

lines.speaker: dynamo
lines.transcription: Can you elaborate?

lines.speaker: yamato
lines.transcription: No.

conversations.summary: Yamato's ancient sword discussed, details withheld.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: Why are you carrying a pillow?

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: I get sleepy.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: Oh god, I'm gonna die.

conversations.summary: Rem carries pillow because he gets sleepy.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: This is no place for a child.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: Probably not.  That's why I'm going to follow you.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: Please don't.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: You can be my big brother that stabs people.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: I'm an only child.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: Not anymore!

conversations.summary: Rem insists on following Apollo despite danger.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: How can I get a jacket like yours?

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: Do you have rich parents?

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: No.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: Are you the best at anything?

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: Sleeping.

lines.speaker: apollo
lines.transcription: You're not getting a jacket like this, sorry.

conversations.summary: Rem wants jacket; Apollo sets conditions.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: You're scary.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: Yes.

lines.speaker: rem
lines.transcription: I don't think I like you.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: It's not an unreasonable position.

conversations.summary: Rem dislikes Seven, who accepts it calmly.

lines.speaker: seven
lines.transcription: You have a pillow.

lines.speaker: rem

conversations.summary: Origins mystery interrupted by ritual starting.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Look at us: a couple of walking corpes ready to stare death in the eye

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You seem fairly cheerful about it.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Why not?  It's better than the alternative.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You have no issues with being a grotesque mirror of humanity.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: My friend: we were gifted unnatural life.  Don't squander it, travel.  Write a book.  Find love.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm not taking advice from someone wearing a parka in New York.

conversations.summary: Embracing life despite feeling like walking corpses.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Do you have a plan for the battle, my morose friend.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: My plans tend to devolve into me throwing my body at my enemies until we all drown in blood.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: That doesn't sound very sustainable.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Ideally I rise like a macabre pheonix, but it doesn't always work  out.

conversations.summary: Victor's grim battle plan and strategy.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: I'll do whatever I can to keep you in the fight Victor.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Thanks Kelvin.

conversations.summary: Kelvin supports Victor to continue fighting.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You, uh, ever find any body parts in the sewers?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: After the ritual, I don't suppose you'd mind if I took a look to see if I could find any matches.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Good point. Yeah, sorry about that.

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Just wondering what my life would look like if I had a mole man's arm.

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You boys ready?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Looking forward to it.

conversations.summary: [Summary not generated]

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: You should fight in the Bear Pit.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm not into getting mauled by grizzlies.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: It's not a literal Bear Pit, it's a fight club where the winner gets the honor of losing to me.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Think I'll pass.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Your loss.

conversations.summary: Invitation to fight in metaphorical bear pit.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Ready to do this Patchwork?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: That's not my name.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Yeah, but there's no way I'm going to remember your name. This is a mnemonic.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Patchwork isn't a mnemonic, it's just you being shitty.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: You got me there, Patches!

conversations.summary: Using nicknames and mnemonics for names.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Here's the plan corpseboy, you draw their fire, and I'll swing in and save the day.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: by the time you "save the day" they'll be laughing over my dead body.

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: Exactly.  That's when The Lash catches them flatfooted.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: What good does that do me?

lines.speaker: lash
lines.transcription: It gives your sad life meaning.  And that should be enough.

conversations.summary: Risky plan to save Victor's life.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Keep the cat away from me.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm not trying to be an asshole, but if a cat pulls my stitches, it's bad for all of us.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Thanks.

conversations.summary: Victor warns cat may harm his stitches.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Did you?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: We're talking after the ritual.

conversations.summary: Conversation about talking post-ritual event.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm fine.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Why? What do you know?

conversations.summary: Victor questions Calico about knowledge.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You're hurting.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: I'm old, of course I'm hurting.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm not talking about your body.

conversations.summary: Grey Talon emotionally hurting beyond physical pain.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: I'll support you the best that I can, but I probably won't be charging in with you.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: That's ok, I'm used to working alone.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: You're not working alone.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Oh. I'll just die alone.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: Basically.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Great.

conversations.summary: Victor and Grey discuss support and solitude.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Heard you hunt monsters.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: I do.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Literally or figuratively.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: It used to be literal.  Then I retired. Now it's more figurative.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: So you just murder people.

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: That's a little reductive.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: is it?

lines.speaker: grey talon
lines.transcription: Yes.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: So you were a gargoyle

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: I mean, I am a gargoyle.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Right, but like, you stone before you were alive

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Yeah.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Do you know who made you?

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Who cares?  I was a hunk of rock.  My life started when I met the Arroyo's, anything before that doesn't really matter to me.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: Are you ok Victor?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: I'm in chronic pain and my body is stitched together from coprses I can't identify.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: That sounds pretty bad.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: It's not great.

conversations.summary: Victor describes severe chronic pain condition.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: So Victor.  I'm not sure what your plans are after the ritual…

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Depends on what I glean from The Patron.  Could be anything from doing research, to traveling on a bus, to shaking down a mass murderer.

lines.speaker: ivy
lines.transcription: I was talking about dinner.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Oh.  I dunno, get a hotdog?

conversations.summary: Discussing post-ritual plans and dinner options.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Thanks.

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: Your welcome.

lines.transcription: Whoever stitched you up did a shit job.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: You think you could do it better?

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: Of course I could do it better.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Didn't realize you were a doctor.

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: I'm not, I'm someone who actually knows how to sew.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Then fix me up after the ritual.

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: You walk one of my shows then sure.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Sure.

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: Really?

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Why not, what else am I gonna do?

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: I don't hate working with you.

lines.speaker: victor
lines.transcription: Damning with faint praise?

lines.speaker: mina
lines.transcription: Take the compliment corpse boy

lines.transcription: Are you ready to summon a god?

lines.speaker: vindicta
lines.transcription: I'm ready for revenge.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Then don't waste your opportunity.

conversations.summary: Summoning a god for revenge opportunity.

lines.transcription: It's unbelieveable... I was killed because people thought I was someone like you, meanwhile you're an actual monster and you get to run around like the belle of the ball.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: That's because I am the belle of the ball.

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: vindicta

lines.speaker: lady geist

conversations.summary: Seeking mutual support amid uncertain futures.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: I'm assuming there will be a celebration at Jezebel's after we win?

lines.speaker: infernus

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: You're doing it right now.

lines.speaker: infernus
lines.transcription: He's a good kid.

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: Ettrick.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Yes.  Ettrick.

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: I don't know.  I tried to find him when I returned to New York but it's like he's vanished.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Well he was rather rude so I say good riddence

lines.speaker: kelvin
lines.transcription: I wish I could share that sentiment

lines.speaker: mo&krill

lines.speaker: mirage

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: It’s a date.

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: shiv
lines.transcription: Just try to keep up Lady Geist.  I'll be accepting your apology later.

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: No Viscous.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: I do!

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: I do.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Wonderful.

lines.speaker: viscous
lines.transcription: Neat!

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Then why are you fighting on their behalf?

conversations.summary: [Not enough transcribed content for summary]

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: You're a rather dour bastard, aren't you?

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Boring people may say that I'm aloof, but at least I know how to have a good time. But you, you just look perpetually miserable.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Whatever you say.

lines.speaker: lady geist
lines.transcription: Don't worry about me, Warden. I know what's at stake.

lines.speaker: lady geist

lines.speaker: wraith
